subject: Computer Networking
title: Dial-Up to Broadband
description: Learn Dial-Up to Broadband in one minute.

hook.text: The screech of a modem connecting. A page loading for thirty seconds. That was dial-up internet in the 1990s.

buildup.text: Dial-up maxed out at 56 kbps. A single song took 15 minutes to download. Video was impossible.

discovery.text: DSL and cable broadband arrived in the early 2000s, offering speeds 10 to 100 times faster than dial-up.

twist.text: Broadband was always-on. No more dialing in, no more busy signals, no more blocking the phone line.

climax.text: Always-on broadband made streaming, gaming, and social media possible. It changed how people lived online.

punchline.text: Broadband didn't just make the internet faster. It made it a lifestyle.

quiz.question: What was the maximum speed of dial-up internet?
